title: No Russian Tunnel to Crimea Act
short_title: No Russian Tunnel to Crimea Act
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subs
plain_summary: This bill says the President must punish foreign people or companies that help build, fix, or take care of tunnels or bridges between mainland Russia and Crimea. The punishments include freezing their money and property in the US and not letting them enter the country. The President can make exceptions if the work is for helping people with humanitarian aid or if it is required by international agreements. The President can also choose not to punish someone if it would be better for US national security.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provi

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Russian Tunnel to Crimea Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress states these facts. In February and March two thousand fourteen, Russia invaded and took over Crimea, which is part of Ukraine according to international law. After taking Crimea, Russia built the Kerch Strait Bridge to connect mainland Russia to Crimea. On February twenty fourth, two thousand twenty two, Russia under Vladimir Putin started a full invasion of Ukraine without being attacked first. Russia has used Crimea as an important part of its invasion of Ukraine by putting Russian soldiers there, storing weapons and ammunition there, and keeping its Black Sea Navy there. In October two thousand twenty three, news reports said that Russian and Chinese business leaders met and talked about building a tunnel from mainland Russia to illegally occupied Crimea.

sections.title: Section 3 Sanctions
sections.text: The President must punish any foreign person who knowingly helps build, maintain, or repair a tunnel or bridge that connects mainland Russia with Crimea. The punishments include blocking assets and visa restrictions. For asset blocking, the President can freeze and stop all transactions involving property and money belonging to the foreign person if that property is in the United States or controlled by US people. For visa restrictions, any foreign person who helps with tunnel or bridge work cannot enter the United States, cannot get a visa to enter the United States, and cannot get any other immigration benefits. Any existing visas must be canceled immediately and automatically cancel any other valid entry documents the person has. There are exceptions to these punishments. The visa ban does not apply if the United States needs to let someone in to follow international agreements like the United Nations headquarters agreement. The punishments do not apply to transactions for selling food, medicine, medical devices, providing humanitarian help, or financial transactions related to humanitarian assistance. The punishments also do not apply to authorized US intelligence, law enforcement, or national security activities. If a court reviews these decisions and the decision was based on classified information, that information can be given to the court privately. The President can use powers from the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out this section. People who violate the rules face the same penalties as those who break the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The President can waive these punishments for a foreign person if the President tells Congress at least fifteen days before the waiver takes effect that the waiver is important for US national security. In this section, appropriate congressional committees means the House Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations. Foreign person means any individual or organization that is not a US person. US person means a US citizen, a permanent resident of the United States, a business organized under US laws including foreign branches of such businesses, or a person in the United States.
